title: APR
description: Examine the components of the cost of borrowing, including annual percentage rate (APR), fixed versus variable interest, length of term, grace period, and additional fees such as late payment, cash advance, and prepayment penalties;

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following helps you determine the *true cost* of borrowing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Choose the best term to fill in the blank.**  

>A(n) \_\_\_\_\_\_ is the best measure of *true cost* of borrowing..

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is APR most often used for?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to determine the true cost of borrowing

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is a fixed interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an interest rate the stays the same for the entire term of the loan
an interest rate that changes based on the market conditions
an interest rate that changes based on how much the lender pays on the loan

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best term to fill in the blank.**  

>A \_\_\_\_\_\_ stays the same for the entire term of the loan.

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is the name for an interest rate that does not change over time?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**What is a variable interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an interest rate that stays the same for the entire term of the loan
an interest rate that changes based on the market conditions
an interest rate that changes based on how much the lender pays on the loan

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the name for an interest rate that can change based on market conditions?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**Choose the best term to fill in the blank.**  

>A \_\_\_\_\_\_ can change based on market conditions

[[☃ radio 1]]

When applying for a loan it is important to understand everything you are agreeing to. 

**What is the meaning of  *loan term*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the interest rate charged on the loan
the length of time it takes to pay off the loan

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best term to fill in the blank.**  

>When you take out a loan, the \_\_\_\_\_\_ is the length of time you have to repay it.

[[☃ radio 1]]

A loan application will list APR, interest rate, loan term, and many other important points.

**What does the *loan term* refer to?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the interest rate charged on the loan
the length of time it takes to pay off the loan

assessment_items.question_content:
You've taken out a loan and your repayment is due today. You're worried that you might not be able to make the payment on time. Your loan may have extra time built in, before you are charged a late fee.

**In your loan paperwork, what should you look for to see how much time you have before you will be charged a late fee?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You've taken out a loan to pay for a new car, and your first payment is due today. However, you've had an unexpected expense and you're worried you won't be able to make the payment on time. 

**What should you look for in your loan paperwork to see if you have extra time before you are charged a late fee?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You've taken out a student loan to pay for college, and your first payment is due today. However, you haven't received your first paycheck from your new job yet and you're worried you won't be able to make the payment on time. 

**In your loan paperwork, where should you look to see how much extra time you have before you are charged a late fee?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

APR helps you understand the total cost of borrowing money because it includes the interest rate and additional fees.  However, some costs are not included in the calculation of the APR, but can also add to the total cost of a loan.

**Select *all* terms that can increase the total cost of a loan.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

When you take out a loan, the APR tells you the total cost of borrowing money, including the interest rate and additional charges. However, some charges are not included in the APR and can add to the total cost. 

**What charges are not included in the APR but can increase the total cost of a loan?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

The APR is designed to help you understand the total cost of borrowing money, but it does not include all charges that could increase the cost of a loan. 

**Select *all* charges that are not included in the APR but could increase the total cost of borrowing money.**


[[☃ radio 1]]

You are a recent college graduate looking for a car loan to purchase your first vehicle. You have two options: Loan A and Loan B. 

| Loan A | Loan B 
-|-|
Interest rate | $5.75\%$ | $5.95\%$ 
APR | $5.9\%$ | $6.1\%$ 
Loan term | $5$ years | $5$ years 
Grace period | $90$ days | $60$ days

**Which loan is a better deal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: APR shows you the *true cost* of borrowing, and it helps you decide on a better deal.
Compare APRs of the loans. The lower the APR the lower the total cost of the loan.
Loan A has lower APR than loan B.
Loan A is a better deal.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are a single parent looking to take out a personal loan to pay for unexpected medical expenses and have two options: loan A and loan B. 

| Loan A | Loan B 
-|-|
Interest rate | $7.45\%$ | $7.25\%$ 
APR | $7.7\%$ | $7.5\%$ 
Loan term | $5$ years | $3$ years 
Grace period | $45$ days | $30$ days

**Which loan is a better deal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: APR shows you the *true cost* of borrowing, and it helps you decide on a better deal.
Compare APRs of the loans. The lower the APR the lower the total cost of the loan.
Loan B has lower APR than loan A.
Loan B is a better deal.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are a small business owner looking to take out a loan to expand your business. You have two options: loan X and loan Y.

| Loan X | Loan Y 
-|-|
Interest rate | $6.75\%$ | $6.55\%$ 
APR | $7.0\%$ | $6.8\%$ 
Loan term | $7$ years | $5$ years 
Grace period | $45$ days | $30$ days

**Which loan is a better deal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: APR shows you the *true cost* of borrowing, and it helps you decide on a better deal.
Compare APRs of the loans. The lower the APR the lower the total cost of the loan.
Loan Y has lower APR than loan X.
Loan Y is a better deal.
